description_long: This is a bronze-colored Corinthian-style helmet with a prominent red crest made of bristly material running from front to back along the top. The helmet features a full face guard with a T-shaped opening for the eyes and mouth, and pointed cheek guards that extend downward. The surface of the helmet is smooth and metallic, with a slightly matte finish and no visible decorative engravings or embellishments. The red crest is thick and bushy, adding a striking visual contrast to the otherwise plain bronze surface. There are no visible ventilation holes, logos, or modern retention systems, giving the helmet a distinctly ancient and utilitarian appearance.
description: A bronze Corinthian helmet with a bold red crest and a smooth, matte metallic finish. The helmet has a full face guard and pointed cheek protectors, with no visible graphics or modern features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the T-shaped face opening, pointed cheek guards, and the red crest centered on top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the helmet's curved profile, the full length of the red crest, and the smooth, unadorned surface.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizing the width and density of the red crest running along the helmet's centerline.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the helmet's rounded back and the continuation of the red crest.
